description:
Goal: make continuation/result delivery a first-class compiler2 native codegen concept instead of callable-entry-only adapter logic plus terminator-local special cases.

Output contract:
- producer delivery shape and consumer delivery shape are derived once from authoritative NativeBody ABI facts
- codegen owns one reusable boundary-adapter inventory for mismatched value-lane / tuple-field delivery seams
- callable entries, resumed receive continuations, and ordinary native return boundaries all consume the same contract

Changes:
- introduce a delivery-shape / delivery-contract model on the compiler2 native codegen surface
- derive it from NativeBody entry_abi / param_reprs / return_abi facts
- generalize callable-return adapters into boundary adapters
- add telemetry for selected delivery path / adapter insertion

Validation:
- focused native codegen tests for direct, adapted, and tuple-field delivery shapes
- telemetry-observed tests for adapter selection
- no ad hoc delivery-shape synthesis outside the model
title: Generalize compiler2 native delivery contracts beyond callable-entry adapters
